Scope: A 6‑month rescue excavation covering a Neolithic henge ditch (extended beyond planning boundary), a waterlogged Roman harbor deposit requiring on‑site conservation, and an unexpectedly dense Medieval cemetery (~400 inhumations).\n\nSchedule Highlights:\n- Weeks 1–4: Site setup, emergency consent for henge extension (weeks 5–7), initial trenching of Neolithic ditch.\n- Weeks 5–7: Henge ditch excavation (0.8 m²/day) and environmental sampling; concurrent preparation of conservation lab for Roman deposit.\n- Weeks 8–14: Osteoarchaeologist works on cemetery (10 inhumations/day, 400 total), DNA sampling per repatriation protocol; parallel Roman harbor work at 0.3 m²/week with conservation buffer.\n- Weeks 15–20: Finish Roman deposit, start artifact processing (30% processed daily, remaining queued).\n- Weeks 21–24: Final cemetery and ditch clean‑up, compile post‑excavation assessment for submission within 12 months.\n\nConstraints:\n• Clay contexts halt when ground frozen/waterlogged – buffer weeks added after any freeze event.\n• Specialist subcontractor windows fixed (osteologist only week 8–14).\n• Artifact processing bottleneck handled by parallel workflow and 6‑week lead time.\n• Regulatory milestone: post‑excavation assessment due within 12 months of project start.
